title: Trading proverbs
setting: Doña Marta is dispensing wisdom in proverbs, as grandmothers do. The skill: understand them and reply in kind.

steps.speaker: Doña Marta
steps.es: Acordate, mijo: camarón que se duerme, se lo lleva la corriente.
steps.en: Remember, dear: the shrimp that sleeps gets swept off by the current.

options.es: Cierto, doña. Al que madruga, Dios lo ayuda.
options.en: True, ma'am. God helps those who rise early.
options.feedback: You answered a proverb WITH a proverb on the same theme — that's true mastery. She'll be delighted.

options.en: What do shrimp have to do with it?
options.feedback: Taking a proverb literally misses its whole point. The image carries the meaning — read past the words.

options.es: Tiene razón, hay que estar atento.
options.en: You're right, one must stay alert.
options.feedback: You understood it — good. But matching her proverb with your own would really land.

steps.speaker: Doña Marta
steps.es: ¡Eso! Veo que ya tenés colmillo. ¿Y este: a buen entendedor...?
steps.en: There you go! I see you've got savvy. And this one: to a good listener...?

options.feedback: You completed the classic refrán perfectly — 'a buen entendedor, pocas palabras.' Flawless.
Reasonable guess, but the set ending is 'pocas palabras.' These are fixed — worth memorizing.
Honest! And fine — but this is among the most common refranes. Tuck it away.

steps.speaker: Doña Marta
steps.es: ¡Ay, este muchacho! Habla mejor que muchos de aquí.
steps.en: Oh, this young man! He speaks better than many from here.

options.es: Aprendí de las mejores, empezando por usted.
options.en: I learned from the best, starting with you.
options.feedback: Gracious and warm, with a little courtly charm — exactly the right note with an elder.

options.es: Es que tuve buenos maestros en la calle.
options.en: It's that I had good teachers in the street.
options.feedback: Humble and true — honoring the everyday people who taught you. Lovely.
Boasting deflates her compliment. Humility is the higher register here.

steps.speaker: Doña Marta
steps.es: Dios te bendiga, mijo. Sos de los nuestros.
steps.en: God bless you, dear. You're one of us.

options.es: Que Dios la bendiga a usted, doña. Gracias por todo.
options.en: May God bless you, ma'am. Thank you for everything.
options.feedback: Returning the blessing in kind is deeply respectful and culturally perfect. A beautiful close.

options.es: Eso es lo más lindo que me han dicho.
options.en: That's the loveliest thing anyone's said to me.
options.feedback: Heartfelt and sincere — receiving her blessing with real emotion. Touching.
Polite, but the moment deserves more warmth than a plain thanks.
